mnemonics: Cats Prefer Fish, Dogs Really Love Bones (Centralized, PKI, Federated, Distributed, Revocation, Lifecycle, Bridged CA)
keyPoints: Centralized (PKI) model relies on a hierarchy of Certificate Authorities (CAs) to issue and manage digital certificates.
Federated model allows different organizations to trust each other's certificates through cross-certification or a common trust anchor.
Distributed (Web of Trust) model uses a decentralized approach where users vouch for each other's keys, popularized by PGP.
commonMistakes: Assuming all public keys are equally trustworthy without verifying the issuing authority or its policies.
Confusing the roles of a Registration Authority (RA) and a Certificate Authority (CA) in a PKI hierarchy.
Failing to properly check a certificate's revocation status (via CRL or OCSP) before trusting it.
